content: VIGOR ADR house style (ADR-0001..0011, applied to ADR-0016): Status / Date / Context / Decision / Alternatives table / Consequences (Positive+Negative subsections) / Citations table. No frontmatter; no docs/adr/README.md index file is maintained; the project does not regenerate an MADR-style index. Decisions with multiple sub-decisions enumerate alternatives Alt-A..Alt-N, each with its own table. Keeps continuity with existing ADR series.

Amend a single row of an Accepted ADR's Alternatives table by writing a new ADR with frontmatter 'refines: ADR-NNNN' rather than 'supersedes: ADR-NNNN'. The old ADR's Status stays 'Accepted' (per MADR rule that Accepted ADRs are immutable except for status-line transitions to superseded/deprecated). The new ADR's Context paragraph names the specific row being revised and explicitly preserves the rest. Used by ADR-0018 to amend ADR-0015's 'Defer .claude-plugin/plugin.json' deferral without invalidating SKILL.md generation, the registry-driven IR pattern, or the dual-package adapter+plugin decision.

name: ci-drift-guard-for-generated-artifacts
description: Generated artifacts (SKILL.md from IR schemas) committed to repo + CI workflow that regenerates and 'git diff --exit-code' on PR. Forces contributors to keep generated files in sync with their inputs. Implemented in .github/workflows/skill-drift.yml per ADR-0015.

content: GitHub Actions workflows in this repo pin third-party actions by full commit SHA with a trailing version comment (e.g., actions/checkout@692973e3...# v4.1.7). New workflows must follow the same supply-chain hygiene.

name: default-deny-across-boundaries
description: Default-deny applied across boundaries — MCP subprocess env (ADR-0013) extends the same posture as ADR-0014's allowed_plugin_factory_prefixes allowlist. Operator declares what crosses the boundary; child gets nothing else (PATH only as the minimum-viable passthrough for stdio CLI shims). When adding a new boundary surface, look for the parallel allowlist primitive on a sibling surface and reference it explicitly in Alternatives Considered.

content: VIGOR ADR house-style is INLINE 'Status: Proposed' / 'Date: YYYY-MM-DD' lines after the H1 (per ADR-0001 and ADR-0016), NOT MADR YAML frontmatter. README claims 'ADR-0017 onward follow MADR' but ADR-0016 (2026-05-15) uses the inline style. When a spec says 'follow ADR-0001/0016/0018 for house style', follow the most recent example matching the spec's named anchors (ADR-0016 here), not the README's general statement. Section order: Context / Decision / Alternatives Considered (table) / Consequences (Positive/Negative/Neutral subheadings) / References (table).

description: Parallel ADR-authoring streams collided on numbers: deployment-strategy stream committed ADR-0012/0013/0019, where 0012/0013 were reserved gap slots and 0019 was claimed by the runtime-strategy stream. Detected post-merge of parent branch; required a follow-up rename branch (VIGOR-dc05) to remap to 0028/0029/0030.
resolution: Cherry-pick the parent branch commit, then 'git mv' each ADR file (preserves rename detection), update H1 line in each file, update internal cross-references between siblings, update docs/adr/README.md index entries, and update any strategy/backlog docs on the same branch. Verify zero remaining old-number references via grep across docs/. Historical references in .seeds/issues.jsonl are out-of-scope (immutable issue history).

name: advisory-convention-adr-vs-core-schema-extension
description: When a future feature would constrain only some adapters (e.g. time-series IRs: needed by audio/animation but not photo/CAD), prefer an advisory convention ADR over a core-schema extension. ADR-0020 documents the body shape (tracks/events/time_position) and patch-objective shape (tracks/<id>:[<start>-<end>] <verb>) as opt-in. This preserves ADR-0011 strict-mode (extra=forbid) for non-adopting adapters and avoids forcing every IR to acknowledge a field it doesn't need. Trade-off: convention is not type-enforced; adoption is a discipline question. Mitigation: cite the ADR from each adopting adapter's adoption brief, and keep the option open to harden into a typed mixin or core field later via a superseding ADR.

name: harness-strategy-three-adr-decomposition
description: Strategic deep-dives that span 8+ orthogonal questions decompose cleanly into 3 ADRs by *concern group* rather than by question count: (1) system architecture (new components and contracts), (2) methodology (datasets, reproducibility, contamination), (3) operational pipeline (gates, sampling, rollback). Mirrors the deployment-strategy stream's ADR triad (0028 cost / 0029 isolation / 0030 posture). Each ADR is mutually-referencing and lands together on one merge cycle.

content: Strategic ADRs draft Status as 'Proposed' with the same date as the parent strategy doc (e.g., docs/adr/0031..0033 all Date: 2026-05-15 matching docs/strategy/harness-v2.md). Mutually-referencing ADRs land together on one merge cycle — never split across commits. The ADR README index entry for the new ADRs notes this explicitly so a future reader can reconstruct the merge unit.

name: runtime-completeness-strategic-doc-anchor
description: VIGOR strategic-doc pattern (mirroring docs/strategy/deployment-and-ops.md from VIGOR-ef9a) extends to runtime concerns: synthesize the 8 production-readiness questions per VIGOR-7724 by classifying each as foundational/tactical and binding foundational ones to ADRs (0031-0034) + Seeds backlog. The doc anchors against an 'existing surface' section with file:line citations, then walks each Q→Decision→Anchors. Resists writing an ADR per Q — Q4 already covered by sibling ADR-0028 reused as-is, Q6/Q7/Q8 deferred to Seeds without ADR. Recommendations table at the end mirrors deployment-backlog.md format.

Cascading replace_all corrupts overlapping integer-range remaps: when renaming a contiguous range like 0031..0034 → 0034..0037, sequential replace_all of each old→new pair causes earlier-replaced strings to be replaced again by a later pair. E.g. 'ADR-0031' → 'ADR-0034' then 'ADR-0034' → 'ADR-0037' turns the original 0031 references into 0037, silently corrupting the file.
resolution: Two-phase sentinel pattern: first map each old number to a unique sentinel that cannot collide with any source or destination token (e.g. ADR-T0034T), then map each sentinel to the final value. Verified clean by grepping for sentinel tokens at the end. Caught early in this branch via 'git diff' inspection after the first replace_all sequence.

name: parallel-stream-adr-collision-rename-from-source-tip
description: Pattern for resolving ADR-number collisions between parallel ADR-authoring streams: branch a rename worker FROM the originating stream's tip commit (NOT from main), use 'git mv' on each colliding file (preserves git rename detection — 96-99% similarity at commit time), update H1 titles + sibling cross-references inside the renamed files (NOT external refs to non-renamed ADRs), update docs/adr/README.md and any strategy docs that reference the renamed ADRs by number or filename, leave the README ADR-number gap unfilled when the merge from main will fill it (e.g. 0030→0034 gap with main supplying 0031/0032/0033). Sibling deployment-adr-rename branch (commit 2a068df) used the same pattern. Acceptance gate: grep for old filename basenames across docs/ returns zero matches.
